_comment: BlueTeam-CTF-Lab :: vulnerability CATALOG (descriptive metadata for docs/training). LAB ONLY.
_note: Runtime toggles + parameters live in attack-scenario-config.json. This file documents each module's risk, MITRE mapping, detection guidance and the CTF question(s) it backs. MITRE IDs flagged 'verify' should be confirmed at attack.mitre.org for your exact scenario.

Modules.Name: Phishing Initial Access Artifacts
Modules.Risk: User receives a malicious attachment and executes it, granting the attacker a foothold on WS01.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: Benign .eml, a harmless attachment placeholder (.docm.txt), recent-files and browser-history endpoint artifacts.
DetectionHints: Event 4688 powershell.exe spawned by Office app

Modules.Name: Kerberoastable Service Account
Modules.Risk: Service account has an SPN and a weak password; any domain user can request its TGS and crack it offline.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: SPN(s) + weak lab password + 'LAB ONLY' description on svc_sql.
DetectionHints: Event 4769 with RC4 (0x17) encryption for the SPN account

Modules.Name: AS-REP Roastable User
Modules.Risk: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled; attacker can request an AS-REP and crack it offline.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: DONT_REQ_PREAUTH flag + weak lab password + 'LAB ONLY' description.

Modules.Name: Vulnerable DACL
Modules.Risk: Low-priv group holds GenericAll/WriteDACL over a privileged service account -> password reset / takeover.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: ACE granting GenericAll to GG_AD_Misconfigured_Operators over svc_backup.

Modules.Name: Excessive / Nested Group Membership
Modules.Risk: Helpdesk group nested into Workstation Admins -> unintended privilege inheritance.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: Group nesting GG_Helpdesk_Operators -> GG_Workstation_Admins (and a secondary path).

Modules.Name: Local Admin Misconfiguration
Modules.Risk: Domain group is local admin on a workstation; via nesting, helpdesk inherits admin -> lateral movement.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: Documentation artifact (and optional real membership if reachable + toggled).

Modules.Name: Fake Credentials in Share
Modules.Risk: Plaintext (fake) credentials in a readable share enable credential reuse.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: old_backup_credentials.txt and related files in the Backup share (all FAKE).

Modules.Name: Inert Suspicious Scheduled Task
Modules.Risk: Legit-sounding task launching PowerShell at startup is a persistence pattern (action is inert here).
Modules.WhatIsStaged: Scheduled task whose action only Add-Content's a heartbeat line; plus evidence artifact.

Modules.Name: Inert Backdoor Domain User
Modules.Risk: Unmanaged service-like account in a privileged group = stealth persistence (never used by the script).
Modules.WhatIsStaged: svc_update user + group membership + creation evidence artifact.

Modules.Name: Suspicious GPO / Delegated ACL
Modules.Risk: Unlinked GPO (deploys nothing) but low-priv group has edit rights -> future code push capability.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: Empty unlinked GPO + edit delegation + evidence artifact.

Modules.Name: Static Lateral-Movement Evidence
Modules.Risk: Reconstructable WS01 -> WS02 -> DC01 movement using svc_backup.
Modules.WhatIsStaged: console_history, remote_commands.log, staged-tool placeholder, admin logon CSV.

Modules.Name: SIEM-Ingestible Kill-Chain Events
Modules.WhatIsStaged: killchain_events.{csv,json,cef,leef} + optional real events to Application/BlueTeamCTFLab.
DetectionHints: Ingest into QRadar/Splunk/Sentinel/FortiSIEM and correlate by time/account/host

Modules.Name: Simulated Firewall / Network Logs
Modules.WhatIsStaged: firewall_flows.{csv,json,cef,leef} with fake lab IPs + suspicious domain.

Modules.Name: MITRE ATT&CK Mapping
Modules.WhatIsStaged: mitre-mapping.md tying each module to tactic/technique/evidence.
